title: salix purpurea subsp. purpurea
intro: Salix purpurea subsp. purpurea, also known as purple willow, is a subspecies of the willow tree that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This deciduous shrub or small tree has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to the salicin content. The health benefits of Salix purpurea subsp. purpurea include relief from pain, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of rheumatism, as well as supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
